about: UCO Bank is engaged in providing wide range of banking and financial services including Retail Banking, Corporate Banking and Treasury Operations.[1]

key_points.raw:
ABOUT [ edit ]

UCO Bank is engaged in providing wide range of banking and financial services including Retail Banking, Corporate Banking and Treasury Operations.[1]

KEY POINTS [ edit ]

Revenue Mix Q3 FY26[1]
Corporate Banking: ~37%
Treasury: ~27%
Retail Banking: ~36%

Key Ratios Q3 FY26[2][3][4]
CRAR - 17.43%
NIM (Global) - 3.08%
Gross NPA - 2.41%
Net NPA - 0.36%
PCR - 97.32%
Cost of Funds - 4.48%

Branch Network[5]
As of Q3 FY26, the bank operates ~3,327 branches. It has a network of ~2,613 ATMs and ~11,128 business correspondents.[6]
Brach Breakup-
Rural: ~34%
Semi-urban: 27%
Urban: 20%
Metro: 19%

Loan Book[7]
As of Q3 FY26, gross advances stood at Rs. ~2,43,594 Cr vs Rs. ~2,08,655 Cr in Q3 FY25. Total domestic advances stood at Rs. ~2,15,244 Cr as of Q3 FY26 and is distributed as:[8]
Corporate: ~33%
Retail: ~30%
MSME: ~21%
Agri: ~16%

Deposits[9]
As of Q3 FY26, total bank deposits were Rs. ~3,10,086 Cr vs Rs. ~2,80,256 Cr in Q3 FY25. Domestic deposits stood at Rs. ~2,91,773 Cr as of Q3 FY26.
CASA: 38.41%
Term deposits: 61.59%

International Business
The bank has 2 overseas branches at Hong Kong and Singapore centers and 1 representative office in Iran. [10] The overseas business accounts for ~8% of the total business in Q3 FY26.[7]

Digital Initiatives
The bank's digital offerings include Merchant Credit Card (MCC) for MSME borrowers, Interoperable Cardless Cash Withdrawal facility, UCO UPI International transaction facilities, etc. [11] [12]As of Q3 FY26, the bank has 20.75 lakh internet banking users and 64.2 lakh mobile banking users.[13]

Investment in Technology
The bank has planned to invest Rs. 1000 Cr in IT advancements. As of Q1 FY25, it has placed order of Rs. 280 Cr for projects like Integrated Treasury, establishment of NOC Center, etc. [14] It will be launching new digital initiatives over the next 12 months (by Q1 FY26). [15]

IMPS Fraud
In Mar 2024, the CBI conducted searches at 67 locations in 7 cities across Rajasthan and Maharashtra concerning suspicious Rs 820 Cr IMPS transactions at UCO bank. Over 8,53,049 IMPS transactions took place in Nov 2023 in which Rs 820 Cr was wrongfully transferred to the accounts of over 41,000 UCO Bank account holders. The bank has recovered 86% of the total amount that was mistakenly credited. [16]

GoI Shareholding
GoI is the majority shareholder with ~91% stake in the Bank as of Dec 2025. From FY16 onwards, GoI has cumulatively infused equity capital of Rs. ~22,600 Cr, including recapitalisation bonds. [17][18]
For FY25, Bank has approved the issuance of 400 Cr equity shares for Rs. 4,000 Cr to reduce government holding to less than 75%. [19]

Regional Rural Bank Update[20]
Bank disclosed that the Government of India has approved the amalgamation of Bangiya Gramin Vikash Bank, Paschim Banga Gramin Bank, and Uttarbanga Kshetriya Gramin Bank into a single entity, West Bengal Gramin Bank, effective May 1, 2025, under the sponsorship of Punjab National Bank; consequently, Paschim Banga Gramin Bank will cease to be an associate of UCO Bank from the effective date, indicating a discontinuation of its RRB association in West Bengal.

Focus
The focus is on CASA and retail term deposits, it strives for low-cost deposits and retail term deposits and intends to reduce dependence on bulk deposits. [21] The Bank continues to focus on its flagship products like KCC, Agri Gold Loans, Horticulture Loans, Financing to SHGs. [22]
